a:r.a:t: This slide gives you the complete tool inventory and RAG pipeline overview in one view. On the left, the 55 tools are organized into 8 functional groups. Orchestration has 14 tools for multi-agent coordination -- spawning workers, messaging between agents, team mailbox channels, and job control. RAG Gateway has 12 tools for document operations -- search, read, compare, extract requirements, consolidate evidence. Analyst has 12 tools for data work -- loading datasets, inspecting columns, executing code in a Docker sandbox, scratchpad operations, and workspace file management. Graph Gateway has 8 tools for knowledge graph queries -- listing indexes, searching graphs, explaining source plans, and importing graph data. Memory has 3 tools for saving, loading, and listing memories. Utility has 3 tools -- calculator, listing indexed docs, and searching skills. Discovery has 2 tools for dynamic tool discovery via MCP. And Skills has 1 tool for executing loaded skills at runtime. On the right, the RAG pipeline shows the five-stage process: query analysis, hybrid retrieval combining vector and keyword search, judge grading where a small fast LLM scores each chunk 0-3, evidence assembly with deduplication and ranking, and synthesis where the main LLM generates a grounded answer with inline citations. Each agent gets a curated subset of tools via its allowed_tools whitelist in its definition file -- no agent has access to everything.
